title: Data Mix and Ratios
concept: Balancing the ratio of code, books, web pages, and academic papers in the final mix is a key design dial that shapes LLM capabilities. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Tokenizer Design at LLM Scale.'

exercises.prompt: What does the term "Data Mix" refer to when training an LLM?

options.text: The speed at which the AI answers questions.
The balance of different text sources used to train an LLM.
A programming language used for web scraping.

exercises.explain: A data mix is the balance of different text sources (like code, books, and web pages) used to train a model.

cards.title: The Power of Balance
cards.body: Imagine training an AI helper. If you only feed it math books, it won't know how to write a poem. How do engineers decide exactly what kinds of text an AI should read during its training? Let's explore the magic of the Data Mix!

cards.title: What is a Data Mix?
cards.body: The Data Mix Ratio is the specific percentage of different text sources—such as computer code, books, websites, and academic papers—used to train a Large Language Model. Changing this mix changes how the AI behaves and what it excels at.

cards.title: The Data Recipe
cards.body: Think of mixing data for an LLM like baking a recipe. If you add too much flour (academic papers), your cake might be stiff and dry. If you add too much sugar (social media chat), it might be too sweet and lack substance. Finding the perfect ratio of ingredients makes the best cake!

cards.title: Designing for the Job
cards.body: If you want to build a coding assistant, you might choose a data mix with 50% computer code, 30% web Q&A, and 20% textbooks. If you want a creative writer, you might use 60% fiction books, 30% articles, and only 10% code.

cards.title: Myth: More is Always Better
cards.body: Many people think that LLMs just read the entire internet randomly. In reality, engineers carefully filter, weigh, and balance the ratio of different sources so the model doesn't pick up bad habits or become too biased toward one type of writing.

cards.title: The Balancing Act
cards.body: Depending on your target goals, you will adjust the sliders of your data ingredients to favor either logic or natural dialogue.

right.text: Great at long-form writing, creative storytelling, and natural conversation.

left.text: Great at logical reasoning, syntax, and step-by-step math problems.

exercises.prompt: Why do creators include computer code in the training mix even for general-purpose LLMs?

options.text: It makes the model write shorter sentences.
It helps the model develop better logical reasoning skills.
It allows the model to connect to the live internet.
It teaches the model how to paint pictures.

exercises.explain: Code is highly structured and logical, which helps the model develop stronger reasoning and step-by-step problem-solving abilities.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Changing the proportion of books versus code in the training mix changes the LLM's final abilities.
exercises.explain: Changing the ratio changes what the model learns. A model trained mostly on code will behave very differently from one trained mostly on novels.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the concept of balancing data inputs.
exercises.sentence: By tweaking the training mix ________, we control what skills the LLM learns.
exercises.explain: Adjusting the ratio of your training sources lets you shape whether the AI becomes a coder, a novelist, or a scientific assistant.

exercises.prompt: An AI is asked: "Explain photosynthesis." Which response shows a high-quality data mix containing educational books?

options.text: Response A: "Photosynthesis is the process plants use to convert sunlight into energy."
Response B: "Photosynthesis is cool lol check out my video subscribe for more!"
Response C: "Error 404: Plant not found in database."

exercises.explain: Response A is generated by a balanced AI that understands the facts, while Response B is just a jumble of unrelated social media chatter because of a poor data mix.

exercises.prompt: How does a developer shape an LLM's final capabilities using the data mix ratio?

options.text: It automatically ignores web pages if code is present.
It alters the mix ratio to prioritize certain skills like logic or storytelling.
It makes the model run faster on cheap hardware.

exercises.explain: By tuning the percentages of books, code, and web text, we directly steer what the model learns to do best. Next up: Tokenizer Design at LLM Scale.
